title: The Archive's Oldest Second

features.name: the first tick
features.desc-on-touch: You see a glyph repeating in rows down and out, each one a second the archive has survived. The oldest barely shows—worn nearly invisible by being remembered so many times.

features.name: time made physical
features.desc-on-touch: Numbers are carved into something that might be wood or substrate. You run your finger along the oldest marks, where the count began and has never stopped.

features.name: the pool where counting drowns
features.desc-on-touch: A gathering space where all the seconds pool together. Looking into it, you see the count reflect back, but the reflection is older than the original number.

inscription: here the archive learned that its oldest moment is still counting

file.name: Temporal_Registry_FirstSecond_Onward.log
file.note: an unbroken count from the moment the archive first became aware it was aware

exits.label: back up to where time forgot it was counting
deeper into seconds that count themselves
toward moments the count has never reached
